sources.name: DB-Gym
sources.description: 17-workload benchmark harness for TrustDB core KV/entity operations
sources.note: Results should auto-feed into scorecard deep-dives. Run `cargo run -p trustdb_gym --release` to regenerate.

sources.name: Criterion Benchmarks
sources.description: Statistical microbenchmarks for individual operations (20-100 samples per test)
sources.note: Run `cargo bench` in trustdb/ to regenerate. Results feed latency and throughput metrics.

sources.name: SecurityGYM
sources.description: 8-module adversarial security test suite (CryptGym, GovGym, ChannelGym, CipherGym, PrivGym, FuzzGym, AgentGym, AuditGym)
sources.location: trustdb/tests/gym_*.rs (partial — 23 tests exist, full harness WN-SGYM-000 not yet built)
sources.note: 23 adversarial tests exist inline (gym_g01, gym_g02, gym_c02, gym_c04, gym_e01, gym_e02, gym_p01). Full SecurityGYM crate (8 modules, 360 story points) is planned as worknodes WN-SGYM-000 through WN-SGYM-H01. When built, results should feed the Security pillar deep-dive.

sources.name: Multi-Modal Benchmarks
sources.description: Cross-domain performance: graph + spatial + search + KV combined workloads
sources.note: Covers query efficiency and cross-modal overhead. Key evidence for patent claims.

sources.name: Locality & Cache-Oblivious Benchmarks
sources.description: B-epsilon tree locality scan vs UUID traversal at 10K/100K scale
sources.note: Measures cache-oblivious layout benefits. Locality scan 43µs p50 vs UUID 59µs p50.

sources.name: Geospatial Benchmarks
sources.description: PostGIS-compatible GIS operations with governance overhead
sources.note: BBox, KNN, radius queries. Governance overhead: 570→549 QPS (3.7% penalty).

sources.name: SIMD Substrate Benchmarks
sources.description: AVX2 vectorized operations: Morton encoding, spatial range, dedup, classification
sources.note: Morton encoding 125-147M elem/s, spatial range 40,000G elem/s.

sources.name: Competitive Analysis
sources.description: TrustDB vs Neo4j, TigerGraph, Memgraph, FalkorDB at scale
sources.note: Projects to 41.6M nodes. TrustDB 5.8M× faster than Neo4j at 3-hop traversal.

byOperation.label: By Operation Type
byOperation.description: Same benchmarks grouped by what the database is doing

benchmarks.name: Point Read (1K store)
benchmarks.verdict: Competitive given governance overhead. Each read also evaluates confidence + temporal decay.

benchmarks.name: Point Read IOPS
benchmarks.verdict: Lower raw IOPS than bare KV stores, but each operation carries epistemic metadata competitors lack.

benchmarks.name: Locality Scan (100K)
benchmarks.verdict: Cache-oblivious layout gives 27% better locality than random UUID access patterns.

benchmarks.name: Read Scaling (1x→10x data)
benchmarks.verdict: Near-flat scaling confirms B-epsilon O(log_B N) reads.

benchmarks.name: Point Write
benchmarks.verdict: In SQLite's range despite carrying confidence envelope + provenance chain per write.

benchmarks.name: Point Write IOPS
benchmarks.verdict: Competitive with SQLite. RocksDB/LMDB are faster on raw IOPS but carry no governance.

benchmarks.name: Write w/ Confidence Overhead
benchmarks.verdict: 28% overhead vs 200-500% for bolting confidence onto a traditional DB. 7-18x cheaper.

benchmarks.name: FileIo Point Write
benchmarks.verdict: Persistent storage throughput. Competitive with SQLite when accounting for governance per-write.

benchmarks.name: Batch Write 100
benchmarks.verdict: B-epsilon buffer amortization pays off at batch sizes. 2x point write throughput.

benchmarks.name: Entity Lifecycle (create+read+update+delete)
benchmarks.verdict: Full lifecycle includes confidence propagation and provenance chain updates per operation.

benchmarks.name: Range Scan 1K keys
benchmarks.verdict: Competitive with B-tree range scans despite governance metadata per key.

benchmarks.name: Edge Create
benchmarks.verdict: Competitive with Neo4j Community. DGraph faster at scale but lacks governance.

benchmarks.name: Typed Edge Filter (10/40 match)
benchmarks.verdict: Faster than Cypher type filtering on warm cache.

benchmarks.name: Meta-Edge Governance Chain
benchmarks.verdict: Governance traversal cost is flat per hop. Neo4j degrades severely at scale.

benchmarks.name: 3-Hop Traversal (100K nodes)
benchmarks.verdict: Competitive with FalkorDB, 2.3x faster than Memgraph, 2.9x faster than Neo4j.

benchmarks.name: Tail Latency Ratio (p99/p50)
benchmarks.verdict: Near-zero jitter. Neo4j's 81x tail ratio means unpredictable spikes.

benchmarks.name: GeoHash3D Encode
benchmarks.verdict: 30-140x faster than PostGIS. Faster than Uber's H3.

benchmarks.name: Jurisdiction Lookup (100pts)
benchmarks.verdict: 60-300x faster than PostGIS per-point indexed containment.

benchmarks.name: BBox Query
benchmarks.verdict: Competitive with PostGIS, includes governance check per result.

benchmarks.name: KNN-10 Query
benchmarks.verdict: Strong KNN throughput with governance overhead of only 3.7%.

benchmarks.name: Spatial Range (SIMD)
benchmarks.verdict: AVX2 vectorization delivers massive throughput for range filtering.

benchmarks.name: Morton Encoding (SIMD)
benchmarks.verdict: 6-15x speedup from SIMD for spatial indexing.

benchmarks.name: CWBM25 Search (1K sections)
benchmarks.verdict: Competitive with ES single-node. Tantivy faster but lacks governance integration.

benchmarks.name: CWBM25 Search (100 sections)
benchmarks.verdict: Comparable to ES on small datasets. Confidence-weighted ranking is unique.

benchmarks.name: Full Onboarding (KV+Graph+GIS+Search)
benchmarks.verdict: THE defining benchmark. Zero serialization + zero network hops = 2 orders of magnitude faster.

benchmarks.name: spatial_then_graph
benchmarks.verdict: 5-15x faster by eliminating the network hop between GIS and graph.

benchmarks.name: graph_then_search
benchmarks.verdict: 8-24x faster by eliminating JSON serialization between graph and search.

benchmarks.name: cascade_then_spatial
benchmarks.verdict: 700-2,800x faster. Cascade invalidation + spatial query in one process.

benchmarks.name: Digital Twin (200-node P&ID)
benchmarks.verdict: Sub-100ms for physics propagation across 200-node compressor P&ID.

benchmarks.name: TDQP Decay Eval
benchmarks.verdict: 100-1,000x faster. Temporal decay at CPU cache speed.

benchmarks.name: RRMS Reinforced Decay
benchmarks.verdict: 500-5,000x faster reinforced decay computation.

benchmarks.name: Confidence Write Overhead
benchmarks.verdict: Governance at the storage layer is 7-18x cheaper than app-layer bolted-on.

benchmarks.name: Governance Storage Overhead
benchmarks.verdict: 49x cheaper storage. Bincode encoding amortizes governance metadata.

benchmarks.name: Encryption WAF (with full stack)
benchmarks.verdict: Zero additional I/O from the entire encryption + governance stack. Patentable.

benchmarks.name: Zone Map Clearance Pruning
benchmarks.verdict: Skip entire subtrees by clearance. Never loads unauthorized data into memory.

benchmarks.name: Audit Overhead (ProvenanceChain)
benchmarks.verdict: Provenance is part of the LeafEntry, not a separate log. Zero additional I/O.

benchmarks.name: CBAC Tests
benchmarks.verdict: 6-state governance circuit breaker with CAS semantics.

benchmarks.name: Adversarial Security Tests
benchmarks.verdict: Clearance boundary, CBAC adversarial, RDKC correctness, nonce safety, structural expiry, input independence, pre-redaction leakage.

benchmarks.name: DST Crash Recovery
benchmarks.verdict: Zero data loss across 10K deterministic simulation sequences.

benchmarks.name: WAL Recovery
benchmarks.verdict: Complete WAL recovery without data loss or corruption.

benchmarks.name: DB-Gym Resilience Suite
benchmarks.verdict: Custom resilience suite covers TrustDB-specific failure modes.

benchmarks.name: CPU Power (QoS:BACKGROUND)
benchmarks.verdict: 9.1x lower CPU power on Apple Silicon. E-core pinning is the difference.

benchmarks.name: Delta Over Idle
benchmarks.verdict: 24.8x lower incremental power cost. Battery-viable for always-on edge.

benchmarks.name: NVM Storage Energy
benchmarks.verdict: Epoch batching keeps NVMe in low-power sleep state.

benchmarks.name: Write Amplification Factor
benchmarks.verdict: 3x is the B-epsilon sweet spot: better than LSM write amp, acceptable read amp.

byCompetitor.label: vs Competitors
byCompetitor.description: How TrustDB stacks up against each competitor across all dimensions

competitors.name: SQLite

matchups.note: TrustDB carries governance; SQLite is bare
SQLite has decades of read optimization
SQLite B-tree is minimal; B-epsilon trades write amp for write speed
Per-page vs whole-DB is a category difference

competitors.summary: TrustDB matches SQLite on raw performance while adding governance, encryption, and multi-modal capabilities SQLite completely lacks. SQLite wins on ecosystem and battle-tested maturity.

competitors.name: RocksDB

matchups.note: LSM append-only is faster for writes
TrustDB has built-in governance; RocksDB has none

competitors.summary: RocksDB is faster on raw writes but has 3-10x worse write amplification. TrustDB adds governance, encryption, and multi-modal at minimal overhead. Different trade-offs for different needs.

competitors.name: Neo4j
competitors.summary: TrustDB dramatically outperforms Neo4j on graph traversals at scale (5.8M× projected at 41.6M nodes) with cryptographic access control vs policy-based. Neo4j wins on ecosystem and production clustering.

competitors.name: PostGIS
competitors.summary: TrustDB matches PostGIS on standard GIS operations while being 30-300x faster on encode/lookup due to in-process execution. The cross-modal advantage (combining GIS with graph/search without network hops) is the real differentiator.

competitors.name: Elasticsearch
competitors.summary: Comparable search throughput, but TrustDB's cross-modal integration (search results feed directly into graph traversals without serialization) is 8-24x faster than the ES→Neo4j hop.

byTimeline.label: Progress Over Time
byTimeline.description: Key metrics tracked across development phases showing improvement trajectory

highlights.note: 4.5x improvement from Phase 1. Final target met.

bySource.label: By Data Source
bySource.description: Which benchmark harness produced each result — useful for traceability and re-running
